text: Standing in these elegantly sterile rooms, surrounded by people and the rhetoric of innovation, the weight of pain—whether physical, emotional, or existential—feels stark against the polished surfaces and curated optimism. The world asks us to celebrate progress, but I cannot ignore the ache I sense in the conversation and, perhaps, within myself. It is a reminder that collective advancement does not erase individual suffering. I feel small waves of empathy and disquiet ripple through me in this space.
